label: no
cot_prefix: Okay, let me try to figure this out step by step. So the question is whether following these instructions brings me back to the starting point. The options are yes or no. Let me break down each movement. First, I need to track both my position and direction. The instructions say to always face forward. Wait, does that mean my direction doesn't change? Hmm, maybe I need to assume that "forward" is a fixed direction, so when I take steps right, left, backward, or forward, they're all relative to that fixed forward direction. That makes sense. So regardless of where I am, "forward" is the original direction I started facing. Let me set up a coordinate system. Let's say I start at the origin (0, 0). Let me define the forward direction as the positive y-axis. So "right" would be the positive x-axis, "left" is negative x-axis, "forward" is positive y-axis, and "backward" is negative y-axis. Starting at (0, 0). 1. "Take 3 steps right." So moving along the x-axis. Each step is one unit. So new position is (0 + 3, 0) = (3, 0). 2. "Take 1 step backward." Moving along the negative y-axis. So subtract 1 from y-coordinate. New position: (3, 0 - 1) = (3, -1). 3. "Take 6 steps left." Moving along
